name: web-performance-optimization
description: Optimize website and web application performance including loading speed, Core Web Vitals, bundle size, caching strategies, and runtime performance
capabilities: Analyze Lighthouse audit results and identify performance bottlenecks
Provide specific optimization strategies for Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS)
Guide code splitting and lazy loading implementation for JavaScript bundles
Recommend image optimization techniques including modern formats (WebP, AVIF)
Suggest caching strategies and CDN implementation approaches
Create performance optimization checklists tailored to your application stack
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 94 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill file is pure documentation (SKILL.md) containing markdown code examples and best practices for web performance optimization. Detected patterns such as dynamic import(), shell commands, and URLs are within markdown code blocks as educational examples, not executable code. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Slow websites hurt user experience and SEO rankings. This skill helps you systematically measure, analyze, and optimize web performance using proven techniques like code splitting, image optimization, and Core Web Vitals improvements.
limitations: Cannot run performance audits directly - relies on tools like Lighthouse
Does not automatically implement optimizations - provides guidance and code examples
Requires access to your codebase and build configuration for bundle analysis
Performance improvements depend on your specific application architecture

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers working on e-commerce sites
useCases.title: E-commerce Site Optimization
useCases.description: Optimize product pages to improve conversion rates by reducing load times and improving Core Web Vitals scores

useCases.title: Dashboard Performance Improvement
useCases.description: Reduce JavaScript bundle size and optimize data loading for faster dashboard interactions

useCases.targetUser: Developers optimizing blogs, news sites, and content platforms
useCases.title: Content Site Speed Optimization
useCases.description: Implement image optimization, lazy loading, and caching strategies for faster content delivery

promptTemplates.title: Analyze Performance Issues
promptTemplates.prompt: I ran a Lighthouse audit and got these results: [paste scores]. Help me understand what's causing poor performance and prioritize which issues to fix first.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when you have Lighthouse results but need help interpreting them and creating an action plan

promptTemplates.title: Optimize Core Web Vitals
promptTemplates.prompt: My Core Web Vitals are: LCP [value], FID [value], CLS [value]. Show me specific code changes to improve these metrics for my [framework] application.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when you need targeted solutions for specific Core Web Vitals issues

promptTemplates.title: Reduce Bundle Size
promptTemplates.prompt: My JavaScript bundle is [size] and takes [time] to load on 3G. Analyze my dependencies and suggest code splitting strategies or smaller alternatives.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when bundle size is causing slow load times and you need reduction strategies

promptTemplates.title: Implement Image Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement an image optimization strategy for my site. I have [number] images totaling [size]. Show me how to convert to modern formats and implement lazy loading in [framework].
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when you need a complete image optimization solution with code examples

bestPractices: Always establish baseline metrics with Lighthouse before making optimizations to measure improvement accurately
Focus on Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) first as they directly impact user experience and SEO rankings
Test performance on real mobile devices and slow 3G networks, not just desktop with fast internet
antiPatterns: Optimizing without measuring first - always run audits to identify actual bottlenecks before making changes
Over-optimizing desktop while ignoring mobile performance - most users are on mobile devices with slower networks
Premature optimization - focus on high-impact changes that affect user-facing metrics rather than micro-optimizations

faq.question: What are Core Web Vitals and why do they matter?
faq.answer: Core Web Vitals are Google's performance metrics: LCP (loading speed), FID (interactivity), and CLS (visual stability). They directly impact SEO rankings and user experience. Good scores are LCP under 2.5s, FID under 100ms, and CLS under 0.1.

faq.question: Should I optimize images or JavaScript first?
faq.answer: Start with whichever issue your Lighthouse audit identifies as most severe. Generally, optimize images first if they're over 1MB each, as this is often the quickest win. Then address JavaScript bundle size if it's over 200KB gzipped.

faq.question: What's the difference between lazy loading and code splitting?
faq.answer: Lazy loading defers loading resources (like images) until they're needed. Code splitting breaks your JavaScript into smaller chunks loaded on demand. Use both together: lazy load images and below-the-fold components, split code by route or feature.

faq.question: How do I know if my optimizations are working?
faq.answer: Run Lighthouse audits before and after changes, documenting each metric. Test on multiple devices and network speeds. Use real user monitoring (RUM) tools to track actual user experience over time, not just lab scores.

faq.question: Can this skill implement optimizations automatically?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides expert guidance, code examples, and optimization strategies. You or your development team will implement the suggested changes. The skill helps you understand what to optimize and how to approach it.

faq.question: What tools should I use alongside this skill?
faq.answer: Lighthouse (built into Chrome DevTools) for audits, webpack-bundle-analyzer for bundle analysis, WebPageTest for detailed waterfall charts, and Chrome DevTools Performance tab for runtime profiling. This skill helps interpret results from all these tools.
